findings_details.title: merged: false contradicted by merged_at: string has no test — silent priority inversion risk
findings_details.body: derivePullRequestState uses Boolean(merged_at) OR-ed with merged===true. When both fields are present and contradict — merged: false, merged_at: '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z' — merged_at wins and the function returns 'merged'. This is probably the right call (GitHub only sets merged_at on truly merged PRs), but the behavior is undocumented in tests. A future maintainer who sees the OR and thinks 'merged: false should take priority' would break list-endpoint callers silently. Test is missing: derivePullRequestState({ state: 'closed', merged: false, merged_at: '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', draft: false }) should return 'merged' — documenting that merged_at takes precedence over an explicit merged: false.

findings_details.title: Missing test for contradictory merged=false + merged_at set payload
findings_details.body: The new heuristic trusts merged_at over an explicit merged=false (line 54 of github-pr-transform.ts: Boolean(merged_at) short-circuits the merged===true check). This means a payload with {state:'closed', merged:false, merged_at:'2025-01-01T00:00:00Z'} returns 'merged', triggering git branch -D in branch-pruner.ts (line 100) — a force-delete that destroys unmerged local work if it were ever incorrectly triggered. The three new tests cover: merged_at set + merged absent (correct), merged_at null (correct), both absent (correct). None covers merged=false with merged_at set. This is the one combination where the function's precedence decision (merged_at wins) is load-bearing and undocumented by a test. Fix: add an it block — derivePullRequestState({state:'closed', merged:false, merged_at:'2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', draft:false}) should return 'merged' — to document that merged_at is authoritative even when merged is explicitly false. This pins the policy in tests and catches any future regression that flips the OR order.
